digest.component_or_area: parallel-agent auto-lander for main (project infra: Gerrit + GitHub Actions)
propositions: Parallel agent sessions racing to land on fast-forward-only main force manual rebase-retry, burning wall-clock CI and agent attention.
An opt-in Autosubmit label lets agents hand a lease to a bot that lands changes autonomously or returns one actionable outcome without weakening the two-vote gate.
Phase 1 delivers a serial auto-lander that rebases the front submittable change/chain to tip, awaits fresh Verified +1, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA.
The agent contract is one project command returning one typed terminal outcome (merged/needs_rebase/ci_failed/review_failed) so agents never correlate Gerrit votes themselves.
The stack is the atomic unit: never partial-land and never evict a change from within a stack; on failure the whole stack is handed back to its owning agent.
Phase 2 adds cross-stack batching that combines non-conflicting changes into one CI pass then casts Verified per member bound to its tested SHA before ancestor-atomic submit.
